description: The Windrush scandal (2018) involved the UK government wrongly detaining or threatening deportation of Commonwealth citizens who had arrived legally before 1973. This tracks the rapid erosion of public trust in the 'Hostile Environment' policy.

key_events.description: Guardian investigative reporting exposes individual cases of wrongful detention.
Home Secretary Amber Rudd resigns over misinformation given to Parliament.
Government formally issues public apology and compensation scheme announcement.

calibration_notes: Tracks how investigative journalism causes a rapid shift in moral consensus regarding bureaucratic state policy.
